rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for interface-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for behavioral contracts, implementations for binding and assignment clauses.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the input AADL model.
Preserve AADL architecture structure during AGREE annex insertion.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE contracts are declared or visible in the target AADL scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the scope.
Implementation-level assignments must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: Type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
